text:
Under a full moon, Lena decided to forage for mushrooms. She had always admired nature's crafts. Her thumb flicked through a mushroom guidebook. She picked a shiny brown one. A wise old owl hooted. "It's just a sapote," Lena murmured, admiring its unusual shape. A raccoon caught her gaze. "A sapote!" He chuckled. Days faded into night. Lena sat by a pool watching stars. Her hand played with the sapote. Then it slipped from her grasp. Out of nowhere a small, shadowy bird swooped down. They exchanged a glance. "Would you keep it?" she asked. "Can you fly?" The bird chirped. "You've got wings?"

They soared together. Under a glittering aurora. Lena laughed.

She went home, a sapote kind of treasure.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
